note: Source URL https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-1973 returns HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.ul.com/resources/your-guide-battery-energy-storage-regulatory-compliance (confirmed live, explicitly references UL 1973, UL 9540, UL 9540A). UL 1973 Edition 3 confirmed published 2022-02-25 (ANSI/CAN/UL 1973:2022). UL 1642 Edition 6 confirmed 2020-09-29. Standard title in row says 'Stationary, Vehicle Auxiliary Power and Light Electric Rail Applications' — confirmed correct per ANSI title. Mandatory_status 'de facto mandatory via NFPA 855 / NEC 706' confirmed accurate.

Source URL https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?productId=UL1973 confirmed live: official UL Standards shop page, shows Edition 3 published 2022-02-25, ANSI/SCC approved. UL 1973 BMS scope (overvoltage, undervoltage, overcurrent, thermal protection as part of module listing) confirmed by UL and industry sources. Mandatory_status 'de facto mandatory as integral part of UL 1973' confirmed. Note: UL 991 referenced as '[UNVERIFIED]' — that flag is appropriate; leave as-is.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-855-standard-development/855 confirmed live (NFPA official page, renders 'NFPA 855 Standard Development'). NFPA 855 2023 edition confirmed published and available (ANSI store, NFPA store, Sandia NNSS). Mandatory_status 'mandatory in US jurisdictions adopting IFC; enforced by AHJ' confirmed accurate — NFPA 855 2023 is widely adopted via IFC. GB 51048-2014 '[UNVERIFIED: latest edition]' flag appropriate — a 2023 revision is in progress per some Chinese standards bodies; leave uncertain flag.

Source URL https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-9540a-test-method returns HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.ul.com/services/ul-9540a-test-method (confirmed live official UL Solutions service page, describes UL 9540A testing service, cites 5th and 6th editions). Standard edition note: row states '4th edition, 2023 [UNVERIFIED]' — this is INCORRECT. Current edition as of 2025-2026 is the 6th edition (UL 9540A:2025, published March 2025); the 5th edition preceded it. The 4th edition was circa 2020. Row's '[UNVERIFIED]' flag is correct to include; edition number needs human correction. Mandatory_status 'de facto mandatory, required by most US AHJs' confirmed accurate.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-70-standard-development/70 confirmed live (NFPA official code development page for NFPA 70/NEC). NEC Article 706 2023 edition confirmed (completely rewritten and reorganised for 2023 cycle per multiple sources). Mandatory_status note: row states 'mandatory — NEC adopted by all 50 US states'; more precisely NEC is a model code adopted by states and local jurisdictions, often on different edition cycles (some still on 2017 or 2020). However, NEC 706 requirement for UL 9540 listing is accurate as stated in the row. The row's phrasing is a common industry simplification; not a material error for this compliance-guidance context. UL 9540 listing prerequisite for NEC 706 confirmed correct.

Source URL https://www.ul.com/services/ul-9540-energy-storage-systems-and-equipment returns HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.ul.com/services/energy-storage-system-testing-and-certification (confirmed live, UL Solutions ESS testing and certification overview page). UL 9540 Edition 3 confirmed published 2023-06-28 (ANSI/CAN/UL 9540:2023), with a 2025-03-07 revision adding Annex H — row cites 'Edition 3, 2023' which is confirmed correct. UL 1741 SB confirmed: 3rd edition published 2021-09-28, includes Supplement B for grid-support functions tied to IEEE 1547-2018. IEEE 1547-2018 confirmed active standard (replaced 2003 edition). UN 38.3 confirmed applicable for lithium battery transport (current Manual of Tests and Criteria Rev.8, 2023, with 2025 amendments). Mandatory_status confirmed accurate.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-855-standard-development/855 confirmed live (same URL as bessus-fire-01, official NFPA page). NFPA 855 2023 edition confirmed. IEEE 1547-2018 confirmed active. UL 1741 SB (2021) confirmed. NEC Article 706 (2023) confirmed. Mandatory_status: 'FERC Order 2222' reference for utility interconnection is accurate (FERC Order 2222 issued 2020 requires RTOs/ISOs to allow aggregated DERs including storage to participate in wholesale markets, but note it applies to wholesale markets, not retail interconnection agreements directly — state-level tariffs govern retail). This is a minor precision issue but not a material error for the guidance context. Row's '[UNVERIFIED: specific state-level requirements]' flag is appropriate.

Source URL https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-9540 returns HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?UniqueKey=44534 (confirmed live, official UL Standards shop page for UL 9540, Edition 3). UL 9540 Edition 3 (2023-06-28) confirmed. NRTL requirement confirmed: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.7 governs NRTL recognition. NEC Article 706 UL 9540 listing requirement confirmed. Mandatory_status 'mandatory — required by most AHJs and NEC 706' confirmed accurate. GB 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2022 cited — GB/T 36558 edition: row says '-2022', which aligns with the 2022 edition (GB/T 36558-2022 superseded the 2017 edition); confirmed.

Source URL https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-9540a returns HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.ul.com/services/ul-9540a-test-method (confirmed live, official UL Solutions service page). Standard description: row describes UL 9540A as a 'four-level (cell, module, unit, installation)' propagation test — confirmed accurate per UL and industry sources. Row references UL 9540A as 'Standard Method of Test' — confirmed (it is a test method, not a listing standard, as correctly stated). IFC 2021 Section 1207 reference for ESS requirements — plausible but unverified directly; the IFC does reference NFPA 855 for ESS in Section 1206/1207 depending on edition. NFPA 855 2023 reference to UL 9540A confirmed (NFPA 855 explicitly cites UL 9540A as the fire propagation test method). Mandatory_status 'de facto mandatory as supporting data for UL 9540 and NFPA 855 / IFC' confirmed accurate. Note: the 6th edition of UL 9540A was published in 2025 (not 4th edition as stated in bessus-fire-02); this row does not specify edition so no correction needed here.
